fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly returns `True` (allowed) instead of `False` (denied).

constraints.intent: When len(recent) equals limit the branch condition should be True (deny), but with > it is False — catches the off-by-one bug.
Asserts that when recent count equals the limit the denial threshold has been reached, exposing the strict-inequality bug.
Checks that the comparison covers the equality case — len(recent) == limit must trigger denial, not allowance.
In the else branch (allowed path) recent count must be strictly less than limit; equality means it should have been denied.
